seoTitle: Osborne Park vs Padbury — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Osborne Park and Padbury. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Osborne Park vs Padbury: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Osborne Park and Padbury.
heading: Osborne Park vs Padbury — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Osborne Park, Western Australia and Padbury, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Padbury has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Osborne Park (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Osborne Park or Padbury safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Osborne Park has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Padbury scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Osborne Park scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Padbury scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Osborne Park or Padbury?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Padbury has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
